stimulus: In 1903, designer Sarah Mae founded the Heath Workshop, which produced furniture using only traditional joinery and locally sourced hardwoods. Historians argue that Mae's rejection of mass-production techniques is ______blank the Heath pieces' use of hand-cut dovetails and slow-aged finishes reminiscent of preindustrial workshops: these meticulously made elements showcase the labor involved.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Evident in" most logically completes the text. The Heath pieces showcase traits associated with preindustrial craft, so the designer's rejection of mass-production techniques is clearly visible—evident in—those very pieces.

Choice A is incorrect. "Uninfluenced by" reverses the relationship; the pieces directly reflect the rejection of industrial methods. Choice B is incorrect. "Scrutinized by" suggests the pieces examine the rejection, which is nonsensical. Choice D is incorrect. "Complicated by" suggests an obstacle; the pieces don't complicate the rejection but illustrate it.
